02/05/2026, 11:35 Gmail \- Save 50% on Gemini API costs with batch requests ![][image1]**Daniel Adersteg \<adersteg.daniel@gmail.com\>** 

**Save 50% on Gemini API costs with batch requests** 

1 message 

**Google AI Studio** \<googleaistudio-noreply@google.com\> 2 May 2026 at 10:34 Reply-To: googleaistudio-noreply@google.com 

To: Adersteg.Daniel@gmail.com 

Email not displaying correctly? View it online 

![][image2]

Save 50% on Gemini API costs with 

batch requests 

Try Batch API 

Hi Daniel, 

If you’re running large-scale data processing or evaluation tasks, you can now save 50% on your Gemini API costs by switching from synchronous calls to the **Gemini Batch API**. 

Designed for high-throughput workloads, it allows you to submit your entire workload in a single job using a JSONL file (up to 2GB of data). 

**Why developers switch to Batch API** 

**50% Cost Savings**: Process your data at exactly half the standard Gemini API price 

for both input and output tokens. 

**Higher Rate Limits**: Bypass the standard rate limits of the REST API to process 

massive datasets in one go, often in minutes. 

**Same High-quality**: Get the same high-quality output and context window as 

synchronous calls. 

Watch visual walk through guide 

https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/?ik=e2986fcfa7\&view=pt\&search=all\&permthid=thread-f:1864068734242054902\&simpl=msg-f:1864068734242054902 1/4  
02/05/2026, 11:35 Gmail \- Save 50% on Gemini API costs with batch requests **Start saving 50% with Batch API in minutes** 

Step 1: Upload your dataset and initiate the async job 

To get started, create a JSONL file with your requests (see the **Batch API docs** for the schema), then use our Python or JavaScript SDK — or a REST API call — to upload 

and start processing. 

from google import genai 

client \= genai.Client() 

\# Upload the batch requests file 

uploaded\_file \= client.files.upload( 

file="batch\_requests.jsonl", 

config={"mime\_type": "jsonl"} 

) 

\# Create a batch job 

batch\_job \= client.batches.create( 

model="gemini-3-flash-preview", 

src=uploaded\_file.name, 

config={ 

"display\_name": "batch-job-1", 

}, 

) 

Step 2: Retrieve your results 

Once the job is complete (often within minutes, guaranteed within 24 hours), you can 

download the results file. The following snippet shows how to download the file once you’ve confirmed the job has succeeded. 

https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/?ik=e2986fcfa7\&view=pt\&search=all\&permthid=thread-f:1864068734242054902\&simpl=msg-f:1864068734242054902 2/4  
02/05/2026, 11:35 Gmail \- Save 50% on Gemini API costs with batch requests 

\# Wait for up to 24 hours and check the state 

batch\_job \= client.batches.get(name=batch\_job.name) 

if batch\_job.state.name \== "JOB\_STATE\_SUCCEEDED": 

file\_content\_bytes \= client.files.download(file=batch\_job.dest.file\_name) 

file\_content \= file\_content\_bytes.decode("utf-8") 

for line in file\_content.splitlines(): 

\# Each line is a JSON object with a response 

print(line) 

This is just a starting point. For detailed guides, error handling, and more advanced 

examples, please check out our **Batch API docs**. 

**Ready to build?**

Explore code samples, technical details, and best practices. 

**Start building**

Learn more about the benefits and pricing of Batch API. 

**Get pricing details** 

Happy building, 

The Google AI Studio team
